title: The Hipaa Privacy Rule Permits Use Or Disclosure
source_filename: The Hipaa Privacy Rule Permits Use Or Disclosure.docx

qas.q: Under HIPAA, a covered entity (CE) is defined as:
qas.a: A health care provider engaged in standard electronic transactions covered by HIPAA

qas.q: Select all that apply: The HIPAA Privacy Rule permits use or disclosure of a patient's PHI in accordance with an individual's authorization that:
qas.a:
-Includes core elements and required statements set forth in the HIPAA Privacy Rule and DoD's implementing issuance
-Is written and signed by the patient

qas.q: An incidental use or disclosure is not a violation of the HIPAA Privacy Rule if the covered entity (CE) has
Which of the following would be considered PHI?
qas.a: An individual's first and last name and the medical diagnosis in a physician's progress report

qas.q: Which of the following statements about the HIPAA Security Rule are true?
Which of the following are fundamental objectives of information security?
Physical safeguards are: [Remediation Accessed
qas.a: Physical measures, including policies and procedures that are used to protect electronic information systems and related buildings and equipment, from natural and environmental hazards, and unauthorized intrusion

qas.q: Technical safeguards are:
qas.a: Information technology and the associated policies and procedures that are used to protect and control access to ePHI

qas.q: Which HHS Office is charged with protecting an individual patient's health information privacy and security through the enforcement of HIPAA?
What of the following are categories for punishing violations of federal health care laws?
If an individual believes that a DoD covered entity (CE) is not complying with HIPAA, he or she may file a complaint with the:
A covered entity (CE) must have an established complaint process.

Which of the following are examples of personally identifiable information (PII)?
Under the Privacy Act, individuals have the right to request amendments of their records contained in a system of records.
The e-Government Act promotes the use of electronic government services by the public and improves the use of information technology in the government
A breach as defined by the DoD is broader than a HIPAA breach (or breach defined by HHS)
When must a breach be reported to the U.S. Computer Emergency Readiness Team?
Which of the following are common causes of breaches?
